top_categories.workout_context: 373 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Cape Rouleur's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
373 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 373 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 19,685ft of climbing on paved — closed roads through wine country and does to your power file on race day.
53ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Cape Rouleur's paved — closed roads through wine country and forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 373 miles.
19,685ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At Cape Rouleur, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Tempo is Cape Rouleur's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 373 miles on paved — closed roads through wine country and. Train it until it's automatic.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Cape Rouleur. Train it until it feels boring.
19,685ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 373 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 373 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and HVLI Extended to prepare you for 373 miles of paved — closed roads through wine country and coastal terrain in Franschhoek, Western Cape, South Africa.
